MEDB51AerobicFitnessNASA Medical Requirements Integration Document for aerobic fitness assessment. Captures VO2max and related cardiovascular fitness data for spaceflight crew members.Questionnairehttps://mitre.org/fhir/space-health/Questionnaire/medb-5-1-aerobic-fitness#0.5.12
overviewMedical Requirements Overview0..1group
overview.mrid-numberMRID Number and Title0..1displayInitial Value: string = MEDB 5.1 Aerobic Fitness Assessment
overview.sponsorSponsor1..1choiceOptions: 1 option
Initial Value: Coding = NASA MRID Sponsor Organizations#exercise-physiology "Exercise Physiology"
overview.disciplineDiscipline1..1choiceOptions: 1 option
Initial Value: Coding = NASA MRID Medical Disciplines#bone-muscle-exercise "Bone/Muscle/Exercise"
overview.purposePurpose/Objectives1..1textInitial Value: string = To assess aerobic capacity (VO2max) using graded exercise testing. Establish baseline fitness and monitor for spaceflight-related cardiovascular deconditioning.
overview.measurement-parametersMeasurement Parameters1..1stringInitial Value: string = VO2max, heart rate, blood pressure, workload, rating of perceived exertion
overview.flight-durationFlight Duration1..1stringInitial Value: string = >= 30 days
preflight-trainingPreflight Training0..1group
preflight-training.descriptionTraining Description0..1textInitial Value: string = Exercise hardware familiarization including T2 treadmill, CEVIS cycle, and ARED resistive exercise device.
preflight-training.durationDuration (minutes)0..1integerInitial Value: integer = 120
preflight-training.scheduleSchedule0..1stringInitial Value: string = L-12 months
preflight-training.completedTraining Completed0..1boolean
preflight-activitiesPreflight Activities0..1group
preflight-activities.descriptionActivity Description0..1textInitial Value: string = Graded exercise test (GXT) on treadmill or cycle ergometer with metabolic gas analysis. Test continues to volitional exhaustion or physician-determined endpoint.
preflight-activities.durationDuration (minutes)0..1integerInitial Value: integer = 45
preflight-activities.scheduleSchedule0..1stringInitial Value: string = L-180 to L-60 days
preflight-activities.hardwareTest Hardware0..1choiceOptions: 2 options

Initial Value: Coding = NASA Medical Facility Locations#jsc "Johnson Space Center"

preflight-activities.test-completedTest Completed1..1boolean
preflight-fitness-resultsPreflight Fitness Results0..1group
preflight-fitness-results.vo2max-absoluteVO2max Absolute (L/min)0..1decimal
preflight-fitness-results.vo2max-relativeVO2max Relative (mL/kg/min)0..1decimal
preflight-fitness-results.max-heart-rateMaximum Heart Rate (bpm)0..1integer
preflight-fitness-results.max-workloadMaximum Workload (Watts or Speed/Grade)0..1string

preflight-fitness-results.resting-bp-systolicResting Systolic BP (mmHg)0..1integer
preflight-fitness-results.resting-bp-diastolicResting Diastolic BP (mmHg)0..1integer
preflight-fitness-results.respiratory-exchange-ratioPeak RER0..1decimal
preflight-fitness-results.test-termination-reasonTest Termination Reason0..1choiceOptions: 6 options
preflight-fitness-results.fitness-categoryFitness Category0..1choiceOptions: 5 options
preflight-fitness-results.notesTest Notes0..1text
inflight-activitiesIn-Flight Activities0..1group
inflight-activities.descriptionActivity Description0..1textInitial Value: string = Periodic submaximal exercise tests using CEVIS or T2 to estimate VO2max changes during mission.
inflight-activities.scheduleSchedule0..1stringInitial Value: string = Flight Day 14, monthly thereafter
inflight-activities.inflight-vo2-estimateIn-Flight VO2max Estimate (mL/kg/min)0..1decimal

postflight-activities.scheduleSchedule0..1stringInitial Value: string = R+5 to R+7 days, R+30 days

postflight-fitness-results.vo2max-absoluteVO2max Absolute (L/min)0..1decimal

postflight-fitness-results.max-heart-rateMaximum Heart Rate (bpm)0..1integer
postflight-fitness-results.percent-changePercent Change from Preflight (%)0..1decimal

Initial Value: Coding = NASA Medical Data Archive Systems#mmis "Medical Mission Information System"

postflight-debrief.completedDebrief Completed0..1boolean

Documentation for this format

Options Sets

Answer options for overview.sponsor

  • https://mitre.org/fhir/space-health/CodeSystem/nasa-mrid-sponsor-cs#exercise-physiology ("Exercise Physiology")

Answer options for overview.discipline

  • https://mitre.org/fhir/space-health/CodeSystem/nasa-mrid-discipline-cs#bone-muscle-exercise ("Bone/Muscle/Exercise")

Answer options for preflight-activities.hardware

  • https://mitre.org/fhir/space-health/CodeSystem/nasa-medical-hardware-cs#quinton-treadmill ("Quinton Treadmill")
  • https://mitre.org/fhir/space-health/CodeSystem/nasa-medical-hardware-cs#cevis ("CEVIS Cycle Ergometer")

Answer options for preflight-activities.location

  • https://mitre.org/fhir/space-health/CodeSystem/nasa-medical-facility-cs#jsc ("Johnson Space Center")

Answer options for preflight-fitness-results.test-termination-reason

  • Volitional exhaustion
  • Target heart rate achieved
  • ECG abnormality
  • Blood pressure response
  • Symptoms
  • Equipment failure

Answer options for preflight-fitness-results.fitness-category

  • Superior
  • Excellent
  • Good
  • Fair
  • Poor

Answer options for postflight-fitness-results.deconditioning-assessment

  • Minimal (<5% decline)
  • Mild (5-10% decline)
  • Moderate (10-20% decline)
  • Significant (>20% decline)

Answer options for data-delivery.archive

  • https://mitre.org/fhir/space-health/CodeSystem/nasa-data-archive-cs#mmis ("Medical Mission Information System")

extension.valueString: The astronaut/crewmember subject of this medical requirement

name: MEDB51AerobicFitness
title: MEDB 5.1 Aerobic Fitness Assessment
description: NASA Medical Requirements Integration Document for aerobic fitness assessment. Captures VO2max and related cardiovascular fitness data for spaceflight crew members.
purpose: To assess pre- and postflight aerobic capacity (VO2max) using graded exercise testing to monitor cardiovascular deconditioning.
copyright: NASA Johnson Space Center - Medical Requirements Integration Documents (MRIDs) JSC28913

initial.valueString: To assess aerobic capacity (VO2max) using graded exercise testing. Establish baseline fitness and monitor for spaceflight-related cardiovascular deconditioning.
VO2max, heart rate, blood pressure, workload, rating of perceived exertion
Exercise hardware familiarization including T2 treadmill, CEVIS cycle, and ARED resistive exercise device.
Graded exercise test (GXT) on treadmill or cycle ergometer with metabolic gas analysis. Test continues to volitional exhaustion or physician-determined endpoint.
Periodic submaximal exercise tests using CEVIS or T2 to estimate VO2max changes during mission.
R+5 to R+7 days, R+30 days
